title: Tetraena fontanesii
intro: Tetraena fontanesii, also known as Desert Rose or Adenium, is a succulent plant species native to the deserts of North Africa and the Middle East. The health benefits of Tetraena fontanesii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as adenin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tetraena fontanesii include teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Tetraena fontanesii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Zygophyllaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the "Greek iron tree" due to its metallic-gray bark.
